actual.name: actual
actual.summary: A system that takes a user's half-formed pursuit, their undone inspirations, and their visualized vision as a three-point anchor, then deploys wandering AI agents across unconstrained domains — math, physics, theater, literature, anything — to surface native and metaphorical work that resonates with that anchor. The system serves two modes: advancing existing published work or research by inheritance, and crystallizing vague new concepts into articulated, living form. Theoretical seeds already in play include chaos theory (unpredictable wandering), Markov chains (state-transition logic with no long-range memory), and the Heisenberg principle (partial knowledge as structural skeleton, not full map).

node_records.embedding_text: a structured input of current pursuit, undone inspirations, and visualized vision that gives an autonomous system enough to navigate against
autonomous explorers sent across all knowledge domains with no predetermined path, guided only by a structural anchor

search_queries: standing on shoulders of giants method
node_records.embedding_text: taking an existing published concept or prior work as a launchpad and pushing it to a next level rather than replicating it
turning half-baked inspirations and inarticulate visions into structured, livable concepts through external resonance
using unpredictable, non-deterministic traversal as a feature rather than a flaw — the path cannot be predicted but can be tracked after the fact
each step in exploration depends only on the current state, not the full history — memoryless transition with local coherence
partial, structural knowledge used as a navigational frame — not a full map but enough of a skeleton to orient movement

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: The core tension is between productive incompleteness and the need for orientation: the user's vision is deliberately half-formed, and the system must not resolve that incompleteness prematurely — it must hold the vagueness as signal, not noise. The wandering is only valuable because it is anchored; the anchor is only valuable because the wandering is genuinely free. A second tension runs between inheritance and departure — the system must honor prior work enough to build on it while preserving the user's impulse to transcend it. The third structural force is the asymmetry between past trackability and future unpredictability: the system can map where it has been but cannot and should not predict where it goes next.

node_records.embedding_text: a state where partial knowledge or half-formed vision is treated as generative fuel rather than a deficiency to be fixed
freedom of movement that remains useful because it is oriented against a fixed structural reference point, not because it is controlled

search_queries: building on versus breaking from precedent
node_records.embedding_text: the pull between honoring a prior structure enough to build from it and the drive to escape that structure entirely
treating an inarticulate or fuzzy input not as noise to be cleaned but as a meaningful structural fingerprint pointing toward something real
a system whose history can be fully reconstructed but whose next state is genuinely unknowable — retrospective clarity without prospective prediction
finding useful matches not by keyword lookup but by structural vibration — a result is valid when its deep pattern rhymes with the anchor, regardless of surface domain

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: determinism collapses novelty; pure randomness collapses utility
retrospective path as the only coherent map
three-point triangulation as minimum viable anchor
mechanism.summary: The causal primitive operating here is: a system whose goal is to produce structurally novel output cannot use deterministic search, because determinism optimizes toward the already-known. But pure randomness produces nothing usable. The resolution is a soft structural anchor — a schema that is rich enough to recognize resonance when the wandering encounters it, but loose enough to permit traversal into domains the user never named. The anchor does not direct the path; it scores the finds. The mechanism compounds: the user's incompleteness is not a bug to be patched before the system runs — it IS the structural shape the system matches against, because only a partial skeleton can resonate with partial skeletons in other domains. Full articulation would narrow the match space to near-zero.

node_records.text: determinism collapses novelty; pure randomness collapses utility
node_records.embedding_text: any system tasked with generating structurally novel output must operate in the space between deterministic search and pure noise — neither extreme produces what the goal requires
a structural reference that evaluates resonance after the fact rather than constraining the path before it — the anchor is a judge, not a leash

search_queries: vague problem statement wider solution space
node_records.embedding_text: a partially specified problem has a larger resonance surface than a fully specified one — full articulation narrows the match space, while structural vagueness keeps it open
when a problem is reduced to its causal and relational skeleton rather than its surface vocabulary, it becomes recognizable in domains that share no nouns with the original

node_records.text: retrospective path as the only coherent map
node_records.embedding_text: in a genuinely non-deterministic traversal system, the only valid map is the one drawn after movement — attempting to pre-map the path destroys the value of the wandering

node_records.text: three-point triangulation as minimum viable anchor
search_queries: minimum viable constraint for open search
node_records.embedding_text: pursuit plus undone inspiration plus visualized vision forms the minimum set of coordinates needed to triangulate a structural shape — fewer points produce a line, not a shape; more points risk over-specification

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I'm currently working on a system that helps people advance their concepts or the work they're currently working on. If there's a case or research that's already published, based on that inheritance, based on that work, we can advance it. If I'm the one who didn't want to be at the same level and I'm just having some similar inspirations and I wanted to take that method or research paper or concept to a next level — how can I do this? I'm making the system like that. Or I'm having a whole new concept: if there's a system I'm building and I wanted to make a feature out of it and I'm having some vague inspirations of that system but I don't know how to articulate it, I don't know how to make it live — there's a system that can do that part of it.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: If a person tells the inspiration, the pursuit, and any unfinished work or unfinished threads in their mind — some of the big inspirations — to the model, then the model has something to anchor against. These three information points — the current pursuit (whether half-baked or not), the undone inspirations, and the vision the user is actually visualizing in their brain — can give enough data to the model to keep intact its score and keep wandering around through the Internet to find native and metaphorical work in the respective domains, whether it's math, physics, psychology, or even theater, literature, anything.

current_map.name: current_map
current_map.content: Based on what I told you about this system, you can't be working on it without some theoretical seeds. I stumbled into many concepts — Heisenberg, Markov chain, chaos theory. Chaos theory is what lets the model wander the Internet unhindered, like total chaos. There's no prediction of where the model will go next, but we can track it. But that track is the past, not the future. We cannot predict where it will end up. That is the spontaneousness I want to give to the model. The Markov chain also plays an important role in my thinking. The same goes with the Heisenberg principle — it doesn't provide a clear understanding, but a half-understanding. Just a structure. Just a skeleton.
